svg.title: Clarity application architecture — HTML input drives all analysis modules
svg.desc: Flowchart showing how a single HTML/EML upload on the main page feeds into a design library and nine analysis modules; module pipelines start when the upload finishes, not when a tab is first opened.

g.text: One HTML or EML input drives everything

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What file formats does the HTML upload support?')
sendPrompt('How should the design name input work?')
sendPrompt('How are saved designs displayed on the main page?')
sendPrompt('How is the design thumbnail generated from HTML?')

svg.text: Clarity modules — start when upload finishes

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me more about the Spelling and Grammar module')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about the Link Analysis module')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about the Spam Detection module')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about the Open Rate and CTR Prediction module')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about the AI Keyword Suggestions module')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about the AI Heatmap module')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about the Design Analysis module')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about the Accessibility module')
sendPrompt('Tell me more about the HTML Analyzer module')

g.text: All modules share the same HTML source
No re-upload needed — each module begins after upload completes

svg.text: Input area — paste raw HTML or upload .html / .eml file
Thumbnail — HTML is rendered to PNG/WEBP on save
Gate — all module tabs are hidden until a design exists
Modules — each starts when upload finishes; tabs show progress and results
Multiple designs can coexist — each has its own analysis state
Click any module above to learn more about it
